name: natural_history
description: Natural History (q.side_bones), played end-to-end at the Takehara museum: take the field brush from Curator Kenji (sets sq_museum_brush, opens the quest stage), donate six bones to Sayuri by the bone statue (donate_bones -> donate_pay: 400 CD + 2 Poke Balls, latches sq_museum_donation_done), then hear Kenji's completed-exhibit line. No earlier-quest tags are assumed; bones are given via rcon instead of brushing suspicious gravel. Re-runs re-pay the 400 CD CobbleDollars (economy balance cannot be reset by the preamble).

steps.comment: -- reset (idempotent re-runs): chain tags + granted items (CD payout is not resettable) --
-- beat 1: Curator Kenji, welcome + the field brush (museum, by the empty plinth) --

expect_screen_text: Welcome to the Takehara Museum of Natural History — three halls, one curator, and a single empty plinth head office relabeled PENDING RE-VERIFICATION. I preferred the old label. It said DONATED BY FRIENDS.
The falls have been cutting these cliffs for ten thousand years. Everything the water uncovers ends up in my strata — what the Company calls pre-verification assets, and I call fossils.

steps.comment: -- beat 2: the dig, faked — six specimen bones straight into the bag --
-- beat 3: Sayuri by the bone statue takes the anatomy-case donation --

expect_screen_text: How goes the brushing? The exhibit hall needs six assorted specimen bones to complete the comparative anatomy case. Bring me six and the museum pays a finders fee — an honest one, in actual money.
Soft pale gravel, low strokes, patience. The water did the hard part ten thousand years ago.

steps.wait_chat: the museum pays its finders fees

steps.comment: -- beat 4: back to Kenji — the plinth is not empty anymore --

expect_screen_text: The comparative anatomy case is complete, and the plinth is not empty anymore — it is full of your bones. That came out wrong. Thank you.
Keep brushing the strata. The water is patient and so am I. And if a fossil turns up, my bench is yours — once.
